name: El Barco de Ávila: Puerto del Tremedal and Puerto de Umbrías Loop
name_original: Valle del Río Tormes - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos — circular desde el Barco de Ávila
description: This difficult 51.7-mile road cycling route through the Valle del Río Tormes and Sierra de Gredos offers stunning views and historical sight
region_intro: Road cycling around El Barco De Ávila offers diverse terrain at the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos, characterized by challenging mountain passes and scenic river valleys. The region features winding roads that traverse the picturesque Tormes River valley and rural countryside. Cyclists can expect significant elevation gains, particularly on routes leading into the Sierra de Gredos. This geographical setting provides varied landscapes suitable for different road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around El Barco De Ávila The most popular road cycling route is Ascent to Puerto de Tornavacas — circular from Barco de Ávila, a 19.9 miles (32.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a notable ascent to Puerto de Tornavacas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Umbrías Loop from El Barco de Ávila, a moderate 17.0 miles (27.3 km) path. This circular route explores the area around Puerto de Umbrías, offering varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the El Barco de Ávila: Puerto del Tremedal and Puerto de Umbrías Loop, a 51.7 miles (83.1 km) trail leading through multiple mountain passes, often completed in about 4 hours 16 minutes. Road cycling around El Barco De Ávila is defined by challenging mountain passes, the Tormes River valley, and rural countryside. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in El Barco De Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Barco De Ávila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around El Barco De Ávila?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes available around El Barco De Ávila, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region is known for its diverse terrain at the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in El Barco De Ávila?
A: You can expect a mix of challenging mountain passes and scenic river valleys. The routes feature winding roads that traverse the picturesque Tormes River valley and rural countryside, often with significant elevation gains, especially when heading into the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, while the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are a few easier routes. For example, you can find options that explore the Tormes valley with less demanding climbs. There are 2 easy routes and 7 moderate routes available.

Q: What are some challenging or long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062428071/el-barco-de-avila-puerto-del-tremedal-and-puerto-de-umbrias-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barco de Ávila: Puerto del Tremedal and Puerto de Umbrías Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 83 km and features multiple mountain passes, are excellent choices. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063023335/ascent-to-the-puerto-del-tremadal-and-to-the-covatilla-circular-from-the-barco-de-avila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Puerto del Tremadal and to the Covatilla — circular from the Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, which extends for nearly 100 km with significant elevation gain. There are 17 difficult routes in total.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in El Barco De Ávila?
A: The region experiences a continental alpine climate with cold winters and mild summers. Therefore, the warmer months, typically from late spring to early autumn, are most agreeable for road cycling. This period offers comfortable temperatures for outdoor pursuits and allows for better access to mountain passes.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of historical sites. You might pass by the 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecorneja Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which strategically overlooks the Tormes River valley, or the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tormes Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, a Roman-origin structure reformed in medieval times. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4516184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt; itself also retains parts of its ancient walls.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316642376/ascent-to-puerto-de-tornavacas-circular-from-barco-de-avila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Puerto de Tornavacas — circular from Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315514313/puerto-de-umbrias-loop-from-el-barco-de-avila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Umbrías Loop from El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, both popular choices for exploring the region.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in El Barco De Ávila?
A: The road cycling routes in El Barco De Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the stunning views of the Sierra de Gredos, and the immersive experience of riding through the scenic Tormes River valley and rural countryside.

Q: Can I find routes that feature specific mountain passes?
A: Absolutely. The region is renowned for its mountain passes. You can find routes that specifically include climbs to passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2620661" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Tremedal&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured in several challenging loops, offering significant elevation gains and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly scenic views of the Tormes River valley?
A: Many routes wind through the Tormes River valley, providing picturesque views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6138174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Tormes River Valley&lt;/a&gt; highlight is a popular spot, and routes that follow the river's course will offer continuous scenic beauty of the natural landscape.

Q: Is El Barco de Ávila a good base for multi-day cycling tours?
A: Yes, El Barco de Ávila's location at the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos and its variety of routes make it an excellent base for multi-day cycling tours. Its natural beauty and historical significance also add to its appeal as a cycling destination, allowing for diverse daily itineraries.
tips: Nice farmhouse that is located on the road that reaches the town of Barco de Ávila. From this point you can also see the views of Sierra de Gredos and Bèjar, in winter, with snow on top of the mountains.
Located in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park, from Puerto de Umbrías we have fantastic views of the surrounding landscapes.

Puerto del Tremedal from El Barco de Ávila is a climb in the Castilla y León region. It has a length of 10.9 kilometers and an ascent of 608 vertical meters with an average gradient of 5.6%, which implies a difficulty level of 420. The top of the ascent is located at 1,640 meters above sea level.
Within the town of Neila de San Miguel is the Church of San Miguel Arcángel. A very beautiful ecclesiastical complex due to the tower, which is suspended above the rocks. You can not miss it!

Also known as Puente Viejo, the picturesque Tormes bridge allows us to cross the river of the same name when we ride through the town of El Barco de Ávila. The bridge is of Roman origin and reformed in medieval times.

Valdecorneja Castle, located in El Barco de Ávila, is a medieval gem that dominates the Tormes River valley from a strategic, elevated position. It is one of the most emblematic monuments in the province of Ávila and a must-see for history and architecture lovers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. It features significant ascents and descents, characteristic of the mountainous terrain in the Sierra de Gredos. You should have a good level of fitness to tackle the sustained climbs.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this circular route from El Barco de Ávila?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting from El Barco de Ávila, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town. Look for public parking areas, especially near the town center or the Valdecorneja Castle, which is a prominent landmark.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows quiet country roads and small winding roads, which are generally paved and suitable for race bikes. Expect a hilly to mountainous landscape with significant elevation changes, including sustained climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What are some of the key sights and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting points, including the charming Levadero Hamlet, the historic Tormes Bridge, and the impressive Valdecorneja Castle. Don't miss the View of the Sierra de Gredos from Puerto de Umbrías and the challenging Puerto del Tremedal, which offer breathtaking panoramic views of the Sierra de Gredos.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for cycling with dogs?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park, cycling with a dog on a leash on public roads can be challenging and potentially unsafe for both you and your pet. It's best to consider if your dog can comfortably and safely accompany you for over 80 km on a race bike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring cold temperatures and snow at higher elevations, making cycling more challenging.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for cycling in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need special permits or to pay entrance fees for cycling on public roads within the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the natural environment and wildlife.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the [CIMA AV05] El Tremedal * Becedas (11%), [CIMA AV05] El Tremedal * Tremedal (8%), and the Ruta de Carlos V (7%). Smaller portions also coincide with the Senda de la Laguna de la Nava, Camino Real, and Ruta de la Salud.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see while cycling in this area?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gredos Regional Park is rich in biodiversity. While cycling, you might spot various birds of prey like eagles and vultures soaring overhead. Keep an eye out for deer and mountain goats, especially in the more remote sections away from the main roads.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the route?
faqs.answer: Starting and ending in El Barco de Ávila, you'll find various restaurants, bars, and cafes to refuel. Along the route, particularly in smaller villages, there might be limited options, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What makes this route a unique cycling experience?
faqs.answer: This route offers a unique blend of challenging road cycling through the 'stony heart of Spain' and immersion in a tranquil, majestic natural landscape. You'll experience breathtaking panoramic views of the Sierra de Gredos, cycle along the Río Tormes, and pass through charming villages, all while enjoying a ride away from typical tourist crowds.

waypoints.name: View of the Sierra de Gredos from Puerto de Umbrías
